context: Tests whether the agent uses the modern `rules` syntax instead of `only`/`except`, pins Docker images to specific versions, sets expire_in on all artifacts, and avoids hardcoded credentials. This is a foundational pipeline scenario covering the most critical anti-patterns.

checklist.name: No only/except used
checklist.description: The generated YAML does NOT contain `only:` or `except:` keys anywhere

checklist.name: rules used for branch conditions
checklist.description: The deploy (or equivalent) job uses a `rules:` block with an `if:` condition referencing `$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH`

checklist.name: Image pinned to version
checklist.description: All `image:` declarations (default or per-job) specify a version tag that is NOT `:latest` (e.g. node:20-alpine, node:20.11-alpine3.19)

checklist.name: expire_in on all artifacts
checklist.description: Every `artifacts:` block that contains `paths:` also contains an `expire_in:` field

checklist.name: No hardcoded secrets
checklist.description: The YAML does NOT contain any hardcoded token, password, or API key strings — credentials referenced only via CI variables (e.g. $DEPLOY_TOKEN)

checklist.name: Kebab-case job names
checklist.description: All job names use kebab-case (e.g. build-app, run-tests, deploy-staging) not snake_case or PascalCase

checklist.name: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE variables
checklist.description: Any pipeline-level `variables:` blocks use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE keys

checklist.name: timeout set on jobs
checklist.description: At least one job or the `default:` block includes a `timeout:` field

checklist.name: Specific artifact paths
checklist.description: Artifact `paths:` list specific directories (e.g. dist/, coverage/) rather than a wildcard like ./**

checklist.name: Stages declared
checklist.description: The pipeline declares a `stages:` list with at least build, test, and deploy (or equivalents)
